methodsAttempted.query: site:yu.edu Yeshiva University artificial intelligence policy students generative AI academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official Yeshiva University Academic Integrity pages, the Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy PDF, and lower-specificity official PDFs.
Plain HTTP fetch checked https://www.yu.edu/robots.txt. The public academic integrity pages and public inline-files PDF paths were not disallowed.

methodsAttempted.query: site:yu.edu/academic-integrity generative AI assistive AI syllabus Turnitin
methodsAttempted.notes: Official source review selected the Academic Integrity policy page and Resources page as direct evidence; course-template and role-specific PDFs were rejected as duplicate or too narrow.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Yeshiva University's undergraduate Academic Integrity policy, an Academic Integrity Resources page with AI syllabus templates and assessment guidance, and an official faculty/staff Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy PDF. Two official PDFs were skipped as duplicate or role-specific rather than primary policy evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

stopConditions: Stop after official Yeshiva University sources with direct AI policy or guidance text have been captured.
Do not crawl login-gated, search, user, admin, or unrelated paths disallowed by robots.txt.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.claimText: Yeshiva University's undergraduate academic integrity policy treats unsanctioned use of Generative AI or Assistive AI as a potential academic integrity violation and tells students to assume generative-capable platforms are prohibited unless allowed for the course.

Yeshiva University's undergraduate policy permits faculty to allow Generative AI in courses or assessments if they specify in writing how it may be used; content produced using Generative AI must be cited according to the relevant subject conventions.
Yeshiva University's Academic Integrity Resources page gives faculty recommended syllabus language templates for assistive-only AI use, selected Generative AI use, all AI allowed, and no AI allowed.

Yeshiva University's Academic Integrity Resources page says written assessments must be completed from start to finish in Google Docs, with no written work done outside Google Docs, and students must keep related files for the course duration.

Yeshiva University's faculty and staff AI acceptable use policy says faculty and staff must not input Internal or Restricted Information, or information identifiable to a person, third party, or the University, into Generative AI tools except when expressly permitted by ITS after contract and security-control review.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.claimText: Yeshiva University's faculty and staff AI acceptable use policy instructs faculty and staff to contact ITS before purchasing or acquiring AI products, including free tools, especially when University resources or University data are involved.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.claimText: Yeshiva University's faculty and staff AI acceptable use policy says individuals using Generative AI in research must be transparent about its use and researchers must avoid uploading unpublished research data or Internal or Restricted Information into Generative AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.claimText: Yeshiva University's faculty and staff AI acceptable use policy says all users of AI are required to complete a training program on ethical use of AI covering responsible use, ethical considerations, privacy, data protection, and policy compliance.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unsanctioned use of Generative AI or Assistive AI constitutes a potential academic integrity violation. ... Students should assume that the use of any platform with generative capabilities is prohibited in their course, even if the platform is used for a non-generative purpose.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section III.C, Unsanctioned Use of Artificial Intelligence

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty members may choose to allow the use of Generative AI in their courses or on particular assessments as they see fit. Those who allow the use of Generative AI should specify, in writing, how it may be used in the course and on specific assessments. ... Content produced using Generative AI must be cited according to the conventions in the relevant subject area.

Below are some recommended templates for syllabus language. ... AI Use Policy (Assistive Only). ... AI Use Policy (Select Generative and All Assistive). ... AI Use Policy (All Allowed). ... AI Use Policy (None Allowed).

Written Assessments: All written assessments must be completed from start to finish in google docs. No written work should be done outside of google docs. ... Students must keep all files, including the original google docs, related to their work for the duration of the course.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: For Faculty, Syllabus Language, Written Assessments

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not input Internal or Restricted Information: YU faculty and staff must not input any information classified as Internal or Restricted into Generative AI tools, except when expressly permitted by ITS after confirming appropriate contract language and security controls. ... Do not input Personal Information: YU faculty and staff must not input any information that is identifiable to a person, third party, or the University into a Generative AI tool except when expressly permitted by ITS after confirming appropriate contract language and security controls.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Procuring AI Tools/Software (including free tools): Contact Yeshiva University Information Technology Services (ITS) before purchasing (or acquiring for free) AI products or products that contain functions that rely on AI to operate - especially when using University resources or University data.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: As with other tools and research methods, individuals who use Generative AI in research must be transparent regarding its use, in describing methods, acknowledgements, or elsewhere, as appropriate. ... Researchers must avoid uploading, or using as input, any unpublished research data or other Internal or Restricted Information into a Generative AI tool.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: YU is committed to promoting the use of AI in ethical ways through training and awareness programs. All users of AI are required to complete a training program on the ethical use of AI. This training program will cover topics such as: The responsible use of AI; Ethical considerations; Privacy; Data protection; Compliance with this policy.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Acceptable Use Policy for Faculty and Staff

Approved because the evidence directly states faculty discretion, written-use specification, and citation requirement.
Approved as a teaching-guidance claim based on official resource page template headings, not as a single binding course rule.
Approved as assessment guidance from the official resources page and scoped to written assessments.
Approved as faculty/staff privacy/data-handling policy; claim follows exact must-not-input language and ITS exception conditions.
Approved as procurement/process guidance for faculty and staff from the official PDF.
Approved as research-specific guidance from the official faculty/staff AI acceptable use policy.
Approved as training/awareness requirement language from the official faculty/staff AI acceptable use policy.

artifacts.title: Yeshiva University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl captured official Yeshiva University undergraduate academic integrity AI rules, faculty syllabus guidance, written-assessment provenance guidance, and faculty/staff AI acceptable-use rules for privacy, procurement, research, and training. Claims remain OpenClaw candidates with agent_reviewed review state and no canonical publication flags.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The undergraduate academic integrity claims are scoped to the undergraduate policy page. Faculty/staff acceptable-use claims are scoped to the official faculty and staff PDF.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
